que: In Repository Pattern, what is the primary goal?
options: abstract data sources behind a clean API
replace testing entirely
avoid understanding requirements
skip state/data modeling

que: Which API/concept is most central to Repository Pattern?
options: manual polling loop for all tasks
random utility class name
Repository
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic

que: What is a recommended best practice for Repository Pattern?
options: mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
ignore edge/error states
merge local/remote sources consistently
optimize before verifying correctness

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing Repository Pattern?
options: keep boundaries clear
follow official docs for API usage
calling network/database directly from UI
write focused tests for behavior

que: For production code, which approach fits Repository Pattern best?
options: Use Repository Pattern with clear state flow and testable logic
Use Repository Pattern while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use Repository Pattern and suppress all warnings
Use Repository Pattern only as theory with no implementation

que: A teammate asks where to start with Repository Pattern. What is best advice?
options: skip running the app after changes
copy an unknown snippet without context
avoid feedback from tests/review
Read official docs (https://developer.android.com/topic/architecture/data-layer) and implement a small sample

que: Which statement about Repository Pattern is most accurate?
options: Repository Pattern is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data
Repository Pattern removes the need for any testing
Repository Pattern is mainly about adding boilerplate
Repository Pattern should be applied identically in every layer

que: In a code review for Repository Pattern, what would you flag?
options: traceable data flow between layers
Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly
explicit modeling of UI/data states
small pure functions with clear names

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using Repository Pattern correctly?
options: A small, working feature that demonstrates Repository in context
a slide deck with no runnable code
an implementation with no error states
a large refactor without tests

que: To learn Repository Pattern deeply, what should you do next?
options: Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using Repository Pattern patterns
skip verification to save time
memorize definitions only
avoid official references
